pattern: Only the run of zero digits hugging the right edge of the string needs to go -- everything before the last non-zero digit is untouched, so this is a single scan (or strip) from the end.
hints: Zeros anywhere in the middle of the number are meaningful and must stay -- you only care about the tail of the string.
Walk in from the right-hand end and stop the moment you hit a digit that isn't '0'.
The answer is the prefix of the string up to and including that first non-zero digit found from the right.

edge_inputs.description: stripping collapses the whole string down to a single leading digit
many trailing zeros collapsing to a single digit
an internal zero must survive even though a trailing zero is removed
single trailing zero on a two-digit number
long string of maximum length ending in many zeros

stress.description: random digit strings of varying length with no leading zero
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    first = str(rng.randint(1, 9))
    rest = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(length - 1))
    return [first + rest]
